title: Tranthellix

{{Quote|Before purchasing a tranthebar control net, it is advised that all potential riders receive full physical and mental examinations to insure they can withstand the stress of tranthebar flights. Mishaps occur even among experienced riders. Amateur racers are required to undertake a four week training program before certification as an amateur racer is issued. Consult the Tranthellix Commerce and Travel Bureau nearest you for more information.|The fiftieth-anniversary edition of ''Galladinium's Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology''|<ref name="Galladinium" />}}
'''Tranthellix''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] situated in the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]]. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the large [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]] [[Bird/Legends|birds]] known as the [[Tranthebar mountain ripper]]s. The world was owned by [[House Petro]] of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region and was the site of [[sky cities]]. Eventually [[Tranthebar mountain ripper|racing]] using the Tranthebar mountain rippers as mounts became established on Tranthellix. Both the rippers and the races were restricted to their homeworld due to House Petro wishing to profit off them, although they became popular in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] due to live [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] broadcasts.

==Description==
Tranthellix was a small, rugged [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Tranthellix system/Legends|Tranthellix system]] of the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], a part of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. Situated in the area of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] known as [[the Tangles]], it was connected via [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] to the [[Osmani system/Legends|Osmani]] and [[Usnia system/Legends|Usnia systems]].<ref name="TEA" /> The world [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] a [[Star/Legends|sun]] and featured [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] on its surface. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Tranthebar mountain ripper]]s, [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]], half-domesticated [[Bird/Legends|birds]] with a [[Limb/Legends|wingspan]] of nearly five [[Meter/Legends|meters]].<ref name="Galladinium" />

==History==
[[File:TranthebarMountainRipper-GalladiniumsFantasticTechnology.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Tranthellix was the homeworld of the Tranthebar mountain rippers.]]
The [[Noble/Legends|nobles]] of the Senex-Juvex sought an exciting and dangerous new sport, with the Tranthebar mountain rippers of Tranthellix being a perfect fit,<ref name="TEA" /> leading to [[Tranthebar mountain ripper racing|races]] with the birds as mounts becoming established relatively recently by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]<ref name="Galladinium" /> and the rippers eventually becoming what Tranthellix was best known for. [[House Petro]], which owned the world, hoped that ripper racing would become a profitable venture and to that end had<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Tranthellix government]] implement strict regulations for exporting rippers off-world.<ref name="Galladinium" />

At some point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'' is set during the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between the events of {{Film|IV}} and {{Film|V}}, which corresponds to some point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the [[Galladinium's 50th anniversary edition|fiftieth-anniversary edition]] of ''[[Galladinium's Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology]]'' noted that it was not expected that the world's government would ever allow a sizeable shipment of rippers off Tranthellix. However, due to live [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] broadcasting, Tranthebar mountain ripper racing nevertheless was becoming popular in certain regions of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. At one point, the individuals [[Joraaz]] and [[Mithran]] and their respective ripper mounts raced each other on Tranthellix.<ref name="Galladinium" />

==Inhabitants==
Tranthellix was under the control of House Petro, one of the aristocratic [[Ancient Houses]] of the Senex-Juvex.<ref name="TEA" /> It was administered by a local government, which also operated instances of the [[Tranthellix Commerce and Travel Bureau]].<ref name="Galladinium" />

==Locations==
Tranthellix was the site of an exotic [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] locale known as the [[sky cities]]. They were recreated as a hyper-realistic simulation via the use of [[holographic generator]]s, [[tactile array]]s, and [[olfactory emitter]]s in the [[adventure room]]s aboard the luxury [[Water/Legends|underwater]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] ''[[Coral Vanda]]'' that operated on<ref name="EGVV" /> the Core Worlds<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Panto system]]</ref> planet [[Pantolomin/Legends|Pantolomin]]<ref name="EGVV" /> by [[4 ABY/Legends|4.5 ABY]].<ref name="4.5ABY">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' dates the events of [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 26|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 26]], which includes a mention of the ''[[Coral Vanda]]'', to 4.5 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after those of {{Film|IV}}, which corresponds to [[4 ABY/Legends|4.5 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Tranthellix was first mentioned in ''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Galladinium" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed it in grid square L-18.<ref name="TEA" />
